sections.paragraph:
ἀπατρικός, ὀν without a father (otherwise unknown and mistakenly restored in Ox 1081, 34 α.[…ι]κ̣ου π̄ρ̄σ̄ as ἀπατρικοῦ πατρός; the ed. cites Dr. Bartlet’s suggestion: ἀγγελικοῦ). Instead read ἀγεννήτου (s. SJCh 90, 12; s. also ἀγέννητος).—DELG s.v. πατήρ.

Ox Ox = POxy, but refers to fragments of documents esp. relating to the lexical data base—List 1

  1. ed. = edited (by), edition

SJCh SJCh = Sophia Jesu Christi—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 99
